lines: +% This is generated by ESQL's PromqlKibanaDefinitionGeneratorTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.
+Returns `k` time series with the highest values, keeping their full label set. When used with `by`, `topk` ranks independently within each group.
+% This is generated by ESQL's PromqlKibanaDefinitionGeneratorTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.

+A `k` close to Integer.MAX_VALUE can trip {{es}}'s circuit breaker (the execution engine allocates a buffer sized to `k`, not to the number of matching series), whereas Prometheus has no equivalent limit. A `without` grouping clause is not yet supported.
+  "description" : "Returns `k` time series with the highest values, keeping their full label set. When used with `by`, `topk` ranks independently within each group.",
+// topk keeps the k series with the highest values, unlike sum/max/etc. it does not reduce them to one value -
+// the winning series keep their full label identity (see the `set_operator_union_heterogeneous_groupings` test
+// above for the same three cluster=prod pods' individual values via `max by (pod)`).
+// k larger than the number of available series: every series is returned, still ranked.
+// topk is evaluated independently at every step of a range query, so the winning series can change from
+// one step to the next as the underlying series values change - here `three` wins the first step and `two`
+PROMQL index=k8s step=5m start="2024-05-10T00:20:00.000Z" end="2024-05-10T00:25:00.000Z" result=(topk(1, network.total_bytes_in{cluster="prod"}))
+// k=0: Prometheus returns an empty result.
+// k far larger than any reasonable series count: still returns every series, still ranked. Note: k values near
+// Integer.MAX_VALUE trip ES|QL's circuit breaker (TopNBy's execution pre-sizes a buffer proportional to k, not
+// to the actual row count) - unlike Prometheus, which has no such ceiling. See TOPK's differenceFromPrometheus.
+// `by (pod)` partitions the ranking by pod across ALL clusters (pod names repeat across prod/qa/staging), so each
+// pod-group can drop a different, independently-ranked loser - unlike `sum(...) by (pod)`, the winners keep their
+// full identity (cluster, region) rather than collapsing to just `pod`.
+         * Support for the PromQL {@code topk()} order-statistic aggregation.
// Last capability should still have a comma for fewer merge conflicts when adding new ones :)
// This comment prevents the semicolon from being on the previous capability when Spotless formats the file.
* Resolves {@link UnresolvedPromqlFunction} nodes inside a {@link PromqlCommand}'s
* plan tree into their concrete plan-node equivalents
public class ResolvePromqlFunctions extends ParameterizedAnalyzerRule<PromqlCommand, AnalyzerContext> {
+                return new AcrossSeriesReduction(unresolved.source(), child, metadata, extraParams, grouping, unresolved.groupingKeys());
return new AcrossSeriesAggregate(unresolved.source(), child, metadata, extraParams, grouping, unresolved.groupingKeys());
-     * Aggregates data within each time series over a time window.
+     * Aggregates samples within each time series over a time window.
-     * Input: Range vector (multiple samples per series over time range)
+     * Input: Range vector, with multiple samples per series over the requested window.
-     * Output: Instant vector (one aggregated value per series)
+     * Output: Instant vector, with one aggregated value per series.
-     * Aggregates data across multiple time series at a single point in time.
+     * Aggregates multiple time series for each evaluation interval.
-     * Input: Instant vector (one sample per series at evaluation time)
+     * Input: Instant vector, with one sample per series for the interval.
+     * Output: Instant vector, aggregated across series.
+     * Grouping: Explicit by labels ({@code by}/{@code without}) or ungrouped.
-     * Transforms each sample in a vector independently (element-wise operations).
+     * Ranks multiple time series for each evaluation interval and keeps a subset of them.
+     * Input: Instant vector, with one sample per series for the interval.
-     * Output: Instant vector (same cardinality, transformed values)
+     * Output: Instant vector, with the selected input series.
+     * Grouping: Explicit {@code by} partitions the ranking; selected series keep their full label identity.
+     * Unlike aggregations, ranking functions preserve the full label identity of selected series.

- *
  • Math: abs(), ceil(), floor(), round(), sqrt(), exp(), ln(), log2(), log10()

- *
  • Trigonometric: sin(), cos(), tan(), asin(), acos(), atan(), sinh(), cosh(), tanh()
-     * Manipulates or queries the label set of time series.
+     * Transforms each sample independently without changing vector cardinality.
+     * Output: Instant vector with the same label sets.
-     * Output: Instant vector (modified labels or label-based filtering)
+     * Grouping: none; each sample is transformed independently.

+ *
  • Math: abs(), ceil(), floor(), round(), sqrt(), exp(), ln(), log2(), log10()
+     * Manipulates, queries, or filters series based on their labels.
* Extracts or computes time-based values from timestamps.
-     * Output: Instant vector (timestamp replaced with time component)
-     * Examples: day_of_month(), day_of_week(), days_in_month(), hour(), minute(), month(), year(), timestamp()
+     * Examples: day_of_month(), day_of_week(), days_in_month(), hour(), minute(),
-     * Operates on native histogram data types.
-     * Converts a scalar to a vector.
-     * Output: Instant vector (single sample with no labels)
+     * Converts a scalar to an instant vector.
-     * Example: {@code vector(42)} produces an instant vector with one sample with value {@code 42}
-     * Converts a vector that contains only a single element to a scalar.
-     * If the vector does not contain exactly one element, {@code NaN} is returned.
+     * Converts a single-element instant vector to a scalar.
+     * If the vector does not contain exactly one element, {@code NaN} is returned.
+     * Produces a scalar without consuming an input argument.
-     * Returns whether this function operates on range vectors.
-     * Returns whether this function operates on instant vectors.
-     * Returns whether this function performs aggregation.
-     * Returns whether this function transforms values element-wise.
-        return this == VALUE_TRANSFORMATION || this == TIME_EXTRACTION || this == METADATA_MANIPULATION;
+     * {@code topk(k, v)} selects the {@code k} series with the highest values rather than reducing the input
+     * vector to a single value, so - unlike {@code sum}/{@code avg}/{@code max}/{@code min} - it has no ES|QL
+     * aggregate function to build: the ctor reference passes the input field through unchanged, and the PromQL
+     * translator (see {@code TranslatePromqlToEsqlPlan#wrapWithTopNBy}) appends the ranking/limiting step itself.
+    public static final PromqlFunctionDefinition TOPK = PromqlFunctionDefinition.def()
+            "Returns `k` time series with the highest values, keeping their full label set. "
+                + "When used with `by`, `topk` ranks independently within each group."
+            "A `k` close to Integer.MAX_VALUE can trip {{es}}'s circuit breaker (the execution engine allocates a "
+                + "buffer sized to `k`, not to the number of matching series), whereas Prometheus has no equivalent limit. "
+                + "A `without` grouping clause is not yet supported."
public static final PromqlFunctionDefinition VECTOR = PromqlFunctionDefinition.def()
public static final PromqlParamInfo SCALAR = PromqlParamInfo.child("s", PromqlDataType.SCALAR, "Scalar value.");
public static final PromqlParamInfo QUANTILE = PromqlParamInfo.of("φ", PromqlDataType.SCALAR, "Quantile value (0 ≤ φ ≤ 1).");
+    public static final PromqlParamInfo K = PromqlParamInfo.of("k", PromqlDataType.SCALAR, "Number of series to keep.");

case AcrossSeriesAggregate agg -> translateAcrossSeriesAggregate(agg, currentPlan, ctx);

/* child already aggregated, no additional `_tsid` grouping needed */

return new TranslationResult(resultPlan, getValueOutput(resultPlan), childResult.pendingFilter(), synthesizedAttributes);
+     * Translates an {@link AcrossSeriesReduction} ({@code topk}): collapse the child to one row per series
+     * (identity grouping - the child's labels pass through unchanged), then rank and keep the top {@code k}.
+     * A {@code by} clause only partitions that ranking; the partition labels are resolved as concrete columns
+     * alongside the identity key so {@link TopNBy} can group on them, without going through the aggregating
